close_reason: Correct: refresh() delegates to get() which re-fetches with fresh session
description: src/lib.rs:311-313 implements SecretStore::refresh as 'self.get().await'. Because get() reuses the cached session OnceCell, refresh() returns the cached secret_id and calls secrets().get() — which DOES re-fetch the value from Bitwarden. So refresh works in the sense that it returns a fresh value, even though it doesn't re-resolve project/secret names. This is correct behavior, but a reader of refresh()'s body cannot tell that without tracing through fetch() and seeing that the .get(secret_id) call is outside the OnceCell init. Add a one-line comment explaining: 'refresh re-fetches the secret value but reuses the cached project/secret IDs. To force ID re-resolution (e.g. after a name change), drop the backend and reconstruct.' This is documentation, not behavior change. Echoes the 'session lifetime' rustdoc on BitwardenBackend (lib.rs:60-66) but is locally absent.
title: refresh() delegates to get() — comment-worthy or genuine no-cache requirement?
